description: List all activities in a schedule. Use the Show Project API when you need the project time zone to interpret UTC timestamps or values expressed in project time.

parameters.name: filters[is_critical]
parameters.description: Filter activities by critical path status in read only project schedule

parameters.name: filters[updated_at][gt]
parameters.description: Filter for activities updated after this timestamp (ISO 8601)

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort by supported fields. Accepts comma separated values to sort by multiple fields. Order is ascending by default, prefix field with '-' for descending

activity_id.description: The unique identifier for the activity

activity_name.description: The descriptive name of the activity

start_date.description: The activity's start date in UTC (ISO 8601 format)

finish_date.description: The activity's finish date in UTC (ISO 8601 format)

duration_unit.description: The unit in which duration is stored (e.g., hours, day)

duration_display_unit.description: The unit in which duration is displayed in the UI

parent_id.description: The ID for the activity's parent activity

ordered_parent_index.description: The order of the activity amongst its siblings

constraint_date.description: The constraint date in UTC (ISO 8601 format)

assigned_company.description: The name of the assigned Company

calendar_id.description: The ID of the assigned calendar

deadline_date.description: The activity's assigned deadline date without timezone (ISO 8601 format)

deadline_variance.description: The difference between the deadline and finish dates in days

category_data.description: The imported activity code details associated with the activity

resource_data.description: The imported resources assignment details associated with the activity

is_critical.description: Describes whether the activity is on the critical path

notes.description: Any notes associated with the activity

created_at.description: The date and time the task was created (ISO 8601 format)

created_by.description: The ID of the user who created the task

updated_at.description: The date and time the task was updated (ISO 8601 format)

updated_by.description: The ID of the user who updated the task

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax or parameters that fail to meet validation requirements.

401.description: Although the HTTP standard specifies "unauthorized", semantically this response means "unauthenticated". That is, the client must authenticate itself to get the requested response.

403.description: The client does not have access rights to the content; that is, it is authenticated, but the server is refusing to give the requested resource. Unlike 401, the client's identity is known to the server.

404.description: The server can not find the requested resource. This can mean either the URL is not recognized, or the resource itself does not exist. Servers may also send this response instead of 403 to hide the existence of a resource from an unauthorized client.
